info.name: Solana Developer Platform Public API
info.description: Public Postman collection generated from the SDP OpenAPI contract. Internal-only endpoint families are excluded.

request.description: Returns service health and build metadata.
Performs readiness checks for downstream dependencies.
Lists API keys for the authenticated organization.
Creates a new API key. The full key is returned once.
Gets API key details by id.
Updates API key attributes. Use null to clear optional fields.
Deactivates (soft deletes) an API key and invalidates it immediately.
Rotates an API key and returns the new key plus the old key grace period.
Initializes wallet signing for the organization or project by creating an active signing configuration.
Ensures the target provider is active and sets it as the default signing provider for the requested scope. Existing on-chain authorities are not rotated.
Lists wallets across all active providers for the requested scope. Use provider to filter to a specific provider.
Provisions a new wallet for the resolved default signing provider configuration, or for an explicitly targeted provider.
Deletes a wallet from the resolved default signing provider configuration, or from an explicitly targeted provider when that provider supports wallet deletion.
Sets the default wallet for the resolved default provider config, or for an explicitly targeted provider.
Returns the resolved default wallet signing configuration for the organization or project. Resolution is DB-backed only (no environment fallback).
Returns active wallet signing configurations for the requested scope plus the resolved default configuration ID.
Aggregates tracked wallet balances for the requested scope. Defaults to aggregating across all active providers for the organization scope.
Returns provider capability metadata, including active/default status for the requested scope.
Returns the resolved wallet public key for transaction construction. Resolution is DB-backed only (no environment fallback).
Submits a memo transaction using the wallet bound to the authenticated API key. This endpoint requires API-key authentication.
Returns wallet metadata, custody provider, public key, and current SOL balance for a specific wallet ID. This endpoint requires authenticated access.
Updates editable wallet metadata such as the display label.
Lists projects in the organization.
Creates a project within the organization.
Gets project details.
Updates project attributes.
Archives a project and prevents future writes.
Lists members assigned to the project.
Adds an organization member to the project.
Updates a project member role.
Removes a member from the project.
Lists API keys scoped to the project.
Creates an API key scoped to the project. The full key is returned once.
Returns all available token templates with their default configuration and supported extensions.
Returns details for a specific token template including default decimals, required extensions, and available overrides.
Lists tokens for the current project or organization.
Creates a token record that can later be deployed to Solana.

Lists issuance transactions across tokens for the current organization or project. Selected-wallet API keys are scoped to their token-readable wallet bindings when walletId is omitted. Use repeated type query parameters, for example type=burn&type=force_burn, to request multiple transaction types.
Gets token details.
Updates stored token fields. For deployed tokens, metadata fields are also written on-chain through the current metadata authority.
Fetches the current on-chain supply and refreshes the cached totalSupply value.
Lists token transactions for an issued token.
Deploys the token to Solana using custody signing.
Builds an unsigned deploy transaction for client-side signing.
Builds an unsigned mint transaction for client-side signing.
Mints tokens using custody signing and submission.
Builds an unsigned burn transaction for client-side signing.
Burns tokens using custody signing and submission.
Builds an unsigned force transfer transaction for client-side signing.
Forces a transfer using permanent delegate authority.
Builds an unsigned force burn transaction for client-side signing.
Burns tokens using permanent delegate authority.
Builds an unsigned authority update transaction for client-side signing.
Updates token authorities using custody signing.
Pauses transfers for a token using the pause authority.
Resumes transfers for a token using the pause authority.
Freezes a token account to prevent transfers.
Unfreezes a token account so it can be used again.
Lists frozen accounts for a token.
Lists allowlist entries for a token.
Adds an allowlist entry for a token.
Removes an allowlist entry from a token.
Retrieves balances for a custody wallet. Wallet lifecycle and provisioning are managed through /v1/wallets.
Retrieves payment policy rules for a custody wallet. Policies are payment controls layered on top of custody-managed wallets.
Updates payment policy rules for a custody wallet. Wallet provisioning and default selection remain in /v1/wallets.

Builds an unsigned transfer transaction for a custody wallet. The source walletId must reference a wallet from /v1/wallets. Private-transfer requests are provider-built here and returned for client review and signing.
Lists payment transfers for the authenticated organization or project scope.

Executes a transfer using server-side custody signing. The source walletId must reference a wallet from /v1/wallets. Private-transfer requests are provider-built, signed by SDP-controlled wallets when required, and submitted on the configured Solana cluster.
Retrieves details for a specific transfer.
Lists SDP-custody outbound recurring payments for the authenticated organization or project scope.

Creates an SDP-custody outbound recurring payment intent from a custody wallet to a counterparty crypto-wallet account. This stores backend state only; activation and collection are added by follow-up endpoints.
Retrieves an SDP-custody outbound recurring payment record.
Lists feature-flagged recurring-payment subscription plans.
Creates a feature-flagged recurring-payment subscription plan record. This stores SDP backend state and Solana subscriptions program identifiers; it does not by itself create the on-chain plan.
Prepares an unsigned Solana subscriptions program create-plan transaction from an SDP subscription plan. This derives and stores the plan PDA but does not submit the transaction.
Retrieves a recurring-payment subscription plan record.
Updates mutable subscription plan fields and on-chain identifiers.
Lists recurring-payment subscriptions.
Creates a feature-flagged recurring-payment subscription record tied to a counterparty. The customer must still sign the Solana subscription authorization transaction.
Prepares the subscriber-signed Solana transaction that initializes the subscription authority and subscribes to the plan. The transaction must still be signed and submitted by the client.
Prepares the subscriber-signed Solana transaction that cancels a subscription. The transaction must still be signed and submitted by the client.
Prepares the subscriber-signed Solana transaction that resumes a canceled subscription before revocation. The transaction must still be signed and submitted by the client.
Prepares the collector-signed Solana subscriptions transfer transaction for an active subscription. The transaction must still be signed and submitted by the collector/fee-payer flow.
Retrieves a recurring-payment subscription record.
Updates mutable subscription status and on-chain identifiers.
Lists collection attempts for a recurring-payment subscription.
Creates a collection-attempt record for a due recurring-payment subscription. Actual Solana settlement is owned by the collection worker/transaction submitter.
Lists generated fiat-to-crypto on-ramp pairs and the providers that support each pair. Supports optional source, destination rail, and provider filters.
Lists generated crypto-to-fiat off-ramp pairs and the providers that support each pair. Supports optional source rail, destination fiat, and provider filters.
Creates a provider-specific on-ramp quote. Hosted providers return a hosted URL; instruction-based providers return manual funding instructions.
Creates a fiat-to-crypto on-ramp session through the selected provider.
Creates a crypto-to-fiat off-ramp session through the selected provider.
Sandbox-only helper that simulates provider-specific transfer completion flows.

item.name: Screen an address across configured compliance providers

request.description: Runs address screening against configured compliance providers and returns provider-level risk scores.
